compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Audit Maintainability finding #2 (MEDIUM). `make typecheck` (`mypy src/rebar`) gates the WHOLE library in CI (the former core-only scope was driven to zero in story urge-abbey-goal), but it runs in default mode — `[tool.mypy]` sets only `python_version`, `ignore_missing_imports`, `follow_imports = silent`. With no `check_untyped_defs` / `disallow_untyped_defs`, the gate catches inconsistencies in EXISTING annotations but lets untyped defs accumulate silently. In-practice discipline is decent (≈48 `type: ignore`, ≈204 `Any` across 77k LOC), so the codebase is close enough that a ratchet is cheap. The repo has a proven ratchet playbook (the BLE001 lint sweep drove an allowlist to empty; the module-size allowlist is shrink-only).

## What / files (config + mechanical fixes only)
- **Phase 1 — `pyproject.toml [tool.mypy]`**: set `check_untyped_defs = true` globally and fix the (expected-small) fallout with minimal, code-tagged annotations. `make typecheck` must stay green.
- **Phase 2 — per-package `disallow_untyped_defs`**: determine the already-clean top-level packages empirically (run `mypy` with `disallow_untyped_defs=true` scoped to each of `src/rebar/{reducer,graph,_store,_io,_snapshot,schemas,grounding,_commands,_cli,llm,review_bot,_engine,_engine_support}` and see which pass with no new errors), enable `disallow_untyped_defs=true` for the clean set via `[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`, and **explicitly enumerate the remaining (still-exempt) packages** in a comment-documented, SHRINK-ONLY list (a package may only ever be REMOVED from the exempt list, never added — the ratchet direction).
- **Guard**: a CI grep (or a short check step) that fails the build if the exempt list grows beyond its committed contents, mirroring the module-size allowlist pattern; document the shrink-only convention in CONTRIBUTING/docs/architecture.md.
- No blanket `ignore_errors`; any new `type: ignore` must carry a specific code (e.g. `type: ignore[arg-type]`).

## Scope
In: `[tool.mypy]` config + mechanical annotation fixes needed to turn each flag on, plus the exempt-list convention/guard. Out: deep type-system refactors (Protocol extraction etc.); reducing the existing `Any` count as a goal in itself; adopting third-party stubs the flags don't force.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `check_untyped_defs = true` is set globally and `make typecheck` is green
- [ ] `disallow_untyped_defs = true` is enabled for an explicit initial set of already-clean packages via `[[tool.mypy.overrides]]`, and the still-exempt package set is explicitly enumerated in pyproject with a comment marking it shrink-only
- [ ] A guard (CI grep or a documented+enforced check) prevents the exempt list from growing; the shrink-only convention is documented
- [ ] No new blanket `ignore_errors`; any added `type: ignore` carries a specific error code
- [ ] `make typecheck` and `make test` green

file_impact.reason: annotation fixes forced by new flags

compiled_state.title: Start the mypy strictness ratchet: check_untyped_defs now, disallow_untyped_defs per package
